title: Python visualize.py: topology_from_states converts IPv6Address to string inconsistently
description: In python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py, topology_from_states() now converts preferred_parent (IPv6Address) to string via 'str(state.preferred_parent) if state.preferred_parent else None'. This creates a Topology (dict[str, str | None]) where parents are strings. However, callers using to_ascii() or other visualization functions now get string parents instead of IPv6Address objects. The type annotation Topology = dict[str, str | None] was already string-based, so this may be correct, but verify that all callers expect string format.
close_reason: Type consistency is correct - Topology type is defined as dict[str, str | None], string conversion is intentional
